description: Voice device-tier warning copy. Authored by I9 (.swarm/research/R9-memory.md §7). Surfaced by I10 in first-run step 2 and the Voice settings tier banner. DO NOT edit the prose without updating R9 §7.

max.header: Your device is in the MAX tier for on-device Eliza.
max.body: Your device can run every local model in parallel: text, voice, ASR, turn detection, speaker recognition, and emotion — all resident at the same time. Expected first-audio latency under 250 ms.
max.banner: All models can be parallelized and held resident.

good.header: Your device is in the GOOD tier for on-device Eliza.
good.body: Your device can keep every local model loaded but will run them one at a time. Text responses, voice synthesis, and ASR are all local; only one heavy model is active per turn. Expected first-audio latency 300–600 ms.
good.banner: All models stay loaded; responses serialize (one heavy model at a time).

okay.header: Your device is in the OKAY tier for on-device Eliza.
okay.body: Your device will load and unload local models as they're needed. Caching does not survive a model swap, so the first response after voice + image-gen at once will be slow. Expected first-audio latency 600–1500 ms. Consider cloud voice for faster turnaround.
okay.banner: Models load/unload per turn. Caching cannot survive a model swap; expect slower latency under voice + image-gen at once.

poor.header: This device is in the POOR tier for on-device Eliza.
poor.body: This device is below the local-voice memory budget. Local responses will be very slow and may fail to load. We recommend Cloud mode — your turn-detection and VAD still run locally for privacy.
poor.banner: This device is below the local-voice budget. Local responses will be very slow. Cloud is recommended.

backend.label: Backend mode
backend.tooltip: Auto consults your device tier to pick the right backend. Force-local overrides the mobile-cloud default. Force-cloud overrides the desktop-local default.

qualityPreset.label: Model quality
qualityPreset.tooltip: Selects which quant variant of the LM + voice models the runtime loads. Max keeps quality high at the cost of more RAM; minimal trades quality for speed and footprint.

maxRamMb.label: Max RAM cap
maxRamMb.tooltip: Hard cap on how much RAM the voice + LM stack may use. Leave empty for the tier's natural total.

allowQuantDowngrade.label: Allow quant downgrade
allowQuantDowngrade.tooltip: When on, Eliza will silently swap to a smaller (less accurate) variant of a model if the bigger one won't fit. When off, Eliza refuses to start a feature that won't fit, with a clear error.

continuousOnBattery.label: Continuous recording on battery
continuousOnBattery.tooltip: Mobile only. When off, continuous mic recording pauses when the device is on battery to save power.

settings.cellularWarning: You're on cellular data. The next model update is ~{{sizeMb}} MB. Download now / Wait for Wi-Fi.
